channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Charter Communications Data Breach Could Impact Nearly 5 Million
item.description: The notorious ShinyHunters extortion group leaked over 42 million records allegedly stolen from Charter in April. The post Charter Communications Data Breach Could Impact Nearly 5 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: MokN Raises $15 Million for Phish-Back Platform
item.description: MokN's platform deploys realistic decoy access points to lure attackers into revealing compromised credentials, enabling organizations to respond before abuse occurs. The post MokN Raises $15 Million for Phish-Back Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Users May Experience Issues Accessing ChatGPT
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Connectors/Apps (Operational) Voice mode (Operational) Search (Operational) Deep Research (Operational) ChatGPT Atlas (Operational) Login (Operational) File uploads (Operational) Conversations (Operational)...

item.title: Gogs Zero-Day Exposes Servers to Remote Code Execution
item.description: The critical-severity issue, assigned a CVSS score of 9.4, is an argument injection flaw that can be exploited by authenticated attackers via pull requests with malicious branch names. The post Gogs Zero-Day Exposes Servers to Remote Code Execution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: unlocking lithium and controlling Ebola
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How a new extraction process could unlock the world’s lithium A new method for extracting lithium could cut costs and emissions from one of the...

item.title: New Russian-Linked GREYVIBE Targets Ukraine with AI-Powered Cyberattacks
item.description: A previously undocumented threat actor dubbed GREYVIBE has been attributed to ongoing and persistent attacks targeting Ukraine and Ukraine-related entities since at least August 2025. GREYVIBE, per WithSecure, is assessed to be a Russian-speaking group operating broadly in the Russian time zone,...

item.title: The deadly Ebola outbreak is proving difficult to control
item.description: The alert was raised on May 5. Four health-care workers in the Ituri Province of the Democratic Republic of the Congo had died from an unknown illness within four days. Rapid response teams were sent to investigate, and tests at a research center in Kinshasa revealed the culprit: the Bundibugyo...

item.title: California Sues 23andMe, Alleging It Failed to Protect User Data in 2023 Breach
item.description: Attorney General Rob Bonta filed the lawsuit against Chrome Holding Co., which 23andMe rebranded under after filing for bankruptcy last March. The post California Sues 23andMe, Alleging It Failed to Protect User Data in 2023 Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Zero Zero Robotics Launches Hover AQUA, the World's First Fully Waterproof Flying Camera
item.description: Zero Zero Robotics has unveiled the Hover AQUA, the world's first fully waterproof flying camera designed for water sports, featuring IP67 dust and water resistance, positive buoyancy for direct water takeoff and landing, and 4K/100fps video recording at under 250 grams.

item.title: BYD Unveils Self-Developed 4nm Autonomous Driving Chip Xuanji A3
item.description: BYD has unveiled its self-developed 4nm automotive-grade autonomous driving chip, the Xuanji A3, along with upgraded intelligent driving systems and a city navigation assistance accident liability policy, marking a major step in automaker chip independence.

item.title: X-Square Robot Unveils WALL-WM, the World's First Event-Level Prediction Embodied AI World Model
item.description: X-Square Robot has released WALL-WM, the world's first event-level prediction embodied AI world model that shifts from frame-by-frame prediction to semantic event understanding, enabling robots to grasp task objectives rather than memorizing pixel sequences.

item.title: What 2,000 Exposed Vibe-Coded Apps Reveal About the Limits of Most Security Stacks
item.description: Shadow AI used to mean employees pasting things they shouldn't into ChatGPT. It now means something bigger: employees building full applications with AI, wiring them into production systems, and publishing them on the open internet. Without Security or IT in the loop. The artifact moved from a...

item.title: Chrome 148 Update Patches 151 Vulnerabilities
item.description: The browser update resolves critical-severity security defects that could potentially lead to remote code execution. The post Chrome 148 Update Patches 151 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
item.description: Pope Leo XIV’s new encyclical on artificial intelligence includes a statement that warrants serious attention from technologists and policymakers: “Technology is never neutral.” Magnifica Humanitas (“Magnificent Humanity”) is a clarion call to all people to act with courage and solidarity as we...

item.title: Malicious Sicoob NuGet Steals Banking Credentials as npm Packages Target Cloud Secrets
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious NuGet package that masquerades as a C# software development kit for Sicoob, one of Brazil's largest cooperative financial systems, to siphon client IDs and PFX certificates. According to Socket, versions 2.0.0 through 2.0.4 of "Sicoob.Sdk"...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: May 29, 08:45 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 29, 08:39 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 29, 08:30 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Xynova Raises Hundreds of Millions in Series A for Dexterous Robot Hand Flex2
item.description: Xynova, the Chinese full-stack dexterous manipulation technology company, has completed a Series A funding round worth hundreds of millions of RMB. The round wa...

item.title: MediaTek Launches Dimensity 8550: A 4nm All-Big-Core Mobile SoC with On-Device AI Focus
item.description: MediaTek has released the Dimensity 8550, a 4nm N4P mobile platform featuring an all-big-core Cortex-A725 CPU architecture, an 880 NPU unit with native Google Gemini Nano V3 support, and comprehensive connectivity features targeting the mid-to-premium smartphone segment.

item.title: Azure - West US 2 (Washington): INC0157956
item.description: May 29, 07:45 UTC Identified - Current status: We've identified an issue with a third-party cloud platform, and we're coordinating with the provider to develop and implement a fix to restore service. We'll provide another update within 60 minutes.Customer experience: Customers hosted in the...

item.title: Cloudflare Notifications issues
item.description: May 29, 07:32 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 29, 07:32 UTC Monitoring - Cloudflare is investigating issues with the Cloudflare Notifications product. Some alerts might be temporarily delayed.Impact is restricted to the notification functionality and it does not affect any...

item.title: Kimsuky Deploys HTTPSpy, Expands Arsenal with HelloDoor and VS Code Tunnels
item.description: The North Korean state-sponsored threat actor known as Kimsuky (aka Velvet Chollima) has been attributed to a fresh set of cyber attacks targeting South Korean military and corporate entities through March and April 2026. "Kimsuky employed a range of tailored social engineering tactics, such as...

item.title: Behavior-Induced Mirror-Prox Temporal-Difference Learning for Faster Off-Policy Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2605.28849v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Gradient temporal-difference methods provide stable off-policy prediction with linear function approximation, but their practical performance is strongly affected by the geometry induced by the auxiliary-variable metric. Existing Mirror-Prox TD...

item.title: Behavior-Aware Auxiliary Corrections for Off-Policy Temporal-Difference Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2605.28855v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Temporal-difference learning with function approximation can be unstable under off-policy sampling. TDC stabilizes off-policy TD through an auxiliary covariance correction, and TDRC further regularizes this correction in a single-timescale recursion....

item.title: The Cognitive Categorical Transformer: Category-Theoretic Inductive Biases for Language Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2605.28864v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Cognitive Categorical Transformer (CCT) is a 306M-parameter architecture that augments a pretrained GPT-2 Small backbone with cognitively grounded components derived from category theory and several inspirations from cognitive science. Under a...

item.title: Ultra-Reduced-Impact-Encased-Logging (URIEL): propose a new method for selective sustainable logging and post-harvest silvicultural treatment in tropical forest using airborne robotics systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.28883v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tropical forests worldwide are under intense deforestation pressure driven by economic and political interests, and scientific evidence suggests this deforestation contributes to climate change. This paper proposes a novel logging method for tropical...

item.title: Review Arcade: On the Human Alignment and Gameability of LLM Reviews
item.description: arXiv:2605.28897v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-generated reviews for scientific papers are gaining considerable traction and are even being officially piloted by major conferences. We have to assume that not only reviewers are using LLM-assistance, but also that authors use LLMs to revise...

item.title: One Mask to Rule Them All: On Hidden Facts after Editing and How to Find Them
item.description: arXiv:2605.28839v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge editing methods such as ROME and MEMIT update factual associations in transformer models by modifying MLP weights. While evaluated mainly by output behavior, their internal mechanism remains underexplored. We investigate whether edits rely...

item.title: Representation Signatures and Risk-Feedback Alignment in LLM Trading Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.28850v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study behavioral alignment and representation dynamics of large language model (LLM) agents in financial decision environments. Using TradeArena, an auditable trading-agent testbed with risk reports, execution simulation, memory, and replayable...

item.title: Mechanistic origins of catastrophic forgetting: why RL preserves circuits better than SFT?
item.description: arXiv:2605.28860v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) frequently induces catastrophic forgetting of prior capabilities. Recent work has shown that reinforcement learning (RL) retains prior capabilities more effectively than supervised fine-tuning (SFT),...

item.title: Molecular Lead Optimization via Agentic Tool Planning
item.description: arXiv:2605.28862v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Drug discovery is a lengthy and resource-intensive process composed of multiple stages. Among these stages, lead optimization plays a critical role in transforming early hit compounds into viable drug candidates. This stage requires improving...

item.title: Self-Play Reinforcement Learning under Imperfect Information in Big 2
item.description: arXiv:2605.28863v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Imperfect-information multiplayer games test whether agents can act under hidden information, sparse rewards, and non-stationary opponents. We study these challenges in Big 2, a four-player imperfect-information card game. We develop a self-play RL...

item.title: StoryMI: Steerable Multi-Agent Therapeutic Dialogue Generation
item.description: arXiv:2605.27393v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can generate fluent dialogue, but prior works lack situational grounding, dynamic strategy control, and evaluation aligned with clinical standards in motivational interviewing (MI). We introduce StoryMI, a multi-LLM agent...

item.title: Debate Helps Weak Judges Reward Stronger Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.27483v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite theoretical promise, debate as a scalable oversight protocol has produced mixed empirical results: gains in some settings, and null effects in others, especially when the judge does not have information hidden from it. We study proposer-critic...

item.title: PAST2HARM: A Simple Adaptive Past Tense Attack for Jailbreaking Multimodal AI
item.description: arXiv:2605.27545v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Jailbreak attacks on multimodal AI systems remain underexplored, even though unsafe image generation can have more severe consequences than unsafe text and current defenses are relatively immature. We introduce PAST2HARM, a simple yet effective...

item.title: Keyphrase Generative Representation of Youth Crisis Conversations Beyond Static Taxonomies
item.description: arXiv:2605.27546v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Crisis Responders (CRs) rapidly assess thousands of youth SMS conversations each year to identify mental health concerns and guide support. Yet youth distress is increasingly expressed through evolving and context-specific language that often does not...

item.title: The Future of Facts: Tracing the Factual Generation-Verification Gap
item.description: arXiv:2605.27564v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language models are becoming the default interface to factual knowledge, yet they often verify outputs more reliably than they generate them. This generation-verification gap (GV-gap) underlies many recent advances in self-improvement and reasoning,...

item.title: Users may encounter issues with conversations
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Conversations (Degraded performance)

item.title: Users may encounter issues logging in or creating an account
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Login (Degraded performance) Login (Degraded performance)

item.title: [AINews] Anthropic raises $965B Series H, releases Opus 4.8 and Dynamic Workflows/ultracode
item.description: Total Anthropic victory!

item.title: Profiling in PyTorch (Part 1): A Beginner's Guide to torch.profiler
Business plan subscription checkout issues on web and mobile web
item.description: Status: InvestigatingSome users may be unable to complete Business plan subscription checkouts on web and mobile web. We are investigating the issue for the listed services.

item.title: Disruption with Copilot Cloud Agent and Copilot Code Review
item.description: May 28, 20:41 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. May 28, 20:06 UTC Update - Open AI models are currently unavailable. We are shifting...

item.title: Billing and subscription management issues
item.description: May 28, 19:23 UTC Resolved - The issue affecting Claude services has been resolved. Impact occurred from 18:27 UTC to 19:05 UTC. May 28, 19:17 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been deployed for the issue affecting Claude services and we are monitoring for recovery. May 28, 19:04 UTC Identified - We are...

item.title: Elevated error rates across multiple services
item.description: May 28, 19:07 UTC Resolved - On May 28, 2026, between 19:07 UTC and 19:16 UTC, multiple GitHub services experienced elevated error rates. This was due to a change that was partially deployed to an authentication service, causing errors for dependent services including the web experience, REST API,...

item.title: The Age of Async Agents — Cognition's Walden Yan & OpenInspect's Cole Murray
item.description: 80% Devin Commits, Spec-to-PR Workflows, Full VMs, Agent Memory, and PMs Shipping Code

item.title: How a new extraction process could unlock the world’s lithium
item.description: Researchers say they’ve found a new way to extract lithium, a crucial metal used in the lithium-ion batteries that power electric vehicles and energy storage arrays. This new technique could be more environmentally friendly and cheaper than existing ones.  The research was published today in...

item.title: Critical Gogs RCE Vulnerability Lets Any Authenticated User Execute Arbitrary Code
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in Gogs, a popular open-source self-hosted Git service, that allows an authenticated user to execute arbitrary code under certain conditions. The security flaw, per Rapid7, is rated 9.4 on the CVSS scoring system. It does not have a CVE...

item.title: Pages upload failures
item.description: May 28, 14:42 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue impacting a subset of Pages uploads. Some customers may experience their uploads returning a 502 error.We are working to analyse and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Cloudflare Tunnel Connectivity Issues via Anchorage (ANC) from 2026-05-14 to 2026-05-28
item.description: May 28, 12:18 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 28, 12:13 UTC Monitoring - Between May 14th and May 28th, 2026, an issue with network routing impacted a subset of Cloudflare Tunnel connections routing through our Anchorage, AK (ANC) point of presence. This issue has been fully...

item.title: The Download: climate tech goes public and the AI Hype Index returns
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Climate tech companies are going public. What’s next? Solar and battery company Solv Energy went public in February, hitting a $6 billion valuation....
